---
title: "Bibliography"
---

Throughout this project, I consulted a wide range of primary and secondary sources. Bibliographies exported from Zotero using APA7th formatting.

[Newspaper articles]

[Digital history methods]

[Fashion movements and fabrics in Australia]

[Other]

[Appendix A - Figure 10]

![](bibliography/line_star.gif){fig-align="center" width="650" height="23"}

## Newspaper articles

Behind the New Look. (1952, May 7). *Australian Women’s Weekly*, 55.

Dior, Australia. (1949, May 1). *Sunday Herald*, 2.

Dior’s Tulip Line For Spring. (1953, March 13). *Advertiser*, 11.

Dress Sense. (1953, September 30). *Australian Women’s Weekly*, 31.

More Paris fashion parades this spring. (1947, April 26). *Australian Women’s Weekly*, 9.

New Paris line. (1953, August 12). *Australian Women’s Weekly*, 16.

New silhouettes at Paris parades. (1947, March 8). *Australian Women’s Weekly*, 39.

Paris Suits And Accessories For Spring Fashion Show. (1947, July 10). *Sydney Morning Herald*, 13.

Princess sees 200 Dior models. (1949, June 1). *News*, 12.

The Dior look. (1953, September 2). *Australian Women’s Weekly*, 28.

Britain "Hippie" piloce in wide drug raids. (1967, November 14). Canberra Times, 4. Cinderella dress. (1967, July 12). Canberra Times, 21.

Crochet dress in gala mood. (1972, April 5). Australian Women’s Weekly, 35.

Crochet work is the fashion rage. (1965, May 13). Canberra Times, 18.

For women. (1971, August 27). Papua New Guinea Post-Courier, 34.

Hippie styles go to the top. (1968, January 30). Canberra Times, 11.

Hippy nite. (1970, February 10). Torres News, 11.

In Europe, it’s in to look hippie. (1971, September 29). Australian Women’s Weekly, 17.

Life style fashion: Are Canberra women sloppy dressers? (1978, September 10). Canberra Times, 13.

Psychedelic prints for 1968. (1968, February 23). Australian Jewish Herald, 17.

The fall of the skirt. (1967, April 27). Canberra Times, 19.

Australian models get the London look. (1966, March 16). Australian Women’s Weekly, 4.

Clothes for mods. (1966, June 29). Australian Women’s Weekly, 61.

Fashion a la Mod. (1965, May 8). Canberra Times, 7.

It's a mod, mod wolrd. (1964, May 20). Australian Women’s Weekly, 4.

Men's fashion revolution. (1965, May 26). Australian Women’s Weekly, 10.

Mini-Prints And Mods. (1965, September 3). Australian Jewish Herald, 16.

Mod climax at Ajax. (1966, August 26). Australian Jewish Herald, 20.

Sydney's mature mods. (1966, July 20). Australian Women’s Weekly, 10.

Tania to judge fashions at the Melbourne Cup. (1966, July 27). Australian Women’s Weekly, 3.

Ugliness—It’s the fun look for models. (1965, December 22). Australian Women’s Weekly, 16.

Clothing for real women’s bodies. (1988, November 6). Canberra Times, 28.

Elegant Lines. (1987, March 15). Canberra Times, 9.

Fashion. (1986, October 5). Canberra Times, 9.

Fashion. (1990, October 19). Australian Jewish News, 30.

Fashion conscious. (1979, June 14). Hamersley News, 21.

Making things up is sew easy. (1986, September 28). Canberra Times, 11.

Power dressing makes the woman compleat. (1989, June 13). Canberra Times, 18.

Tailor-made dressing. (1981, April 15). Australian Women’s Weekly, 92.

Fashion. (1978, May 28). Canberra Times, 29.

Feargal Sharkey’s cycle of survival From punk to solo success. (1986, June 22). Canberra Times, 17.

How Punk has grown up. (1987, September 2). Tribune, 11.

Punk act backfires. (1978, January 11). Papua New Guinea Post-Courier, 7.

Punk goes proper. (1980, June 18). Australian Women’s Weekly, 19.

Punk rock. (1978, March 15). Tribune, 6.

Punk: The way it was. (1978, October 20). Papua New Guinea Post-Courier, 45.

Timespan. (1985, January 23). Canberra Times, 22.

Beach fashion. (1998, November 17). Port Lincoln Times, 16.

Denim’s continual reinvention as fashion. (1994, May 17). Canberra Times, 18.

Desert surf shop riding success wave. (1993, May 4). Canberra Times, 10.

Fashion Casual elegance. (1997, September 5). Australian Jewish News, 3.

Fashion Hang loose. (1994, September 30). Australian Jewish News, 3.

Fashion keeping pace with winter. (1993, May 11). Port Lincoln Times, 19.

Festival in tune with Floriade. (1990, September 18). Canberra Times, 15.

From surf to snow scene. (1992, March 31). Canberra Times, 1.

Hang Ten reaches its 35th “Endless Summer.” (1995, January 11). Hills Messenger, 14.

Hip styles for young fashions. (1996, June 7). Australian Jewish News, 12.

Surf Labels. (1993, March 3). Hills Messenger, 15.

![](bibliography/line_star.gif){fig-align="center" width="650" height="22"}

## Digital history methods

Crane, D., & Bovone, L. (2006). Approaches to material culture: The sociology of fashion and clothing. Poetics, Approaches to Material Culture: The Sociology of Fashion and Clothing, 34(6), 319–333. <https://doi.org/10.1016/j.poetic.2006.10.002>

*Deductive and Inductive Coding in Qualitative Research*. (2024, November 18). Delve. <https://delvetool.com/blog/deductiveinductive>

Drucker, J. (2021). *The Digital Humanities Coursebook: An Introduction to Digital Methods for Research and Scholarship* (1st ed.). Routledge. <https://doi.org/10.4324/9781003106531>

Entwistle, J. (2020). “Power Dressing” and the Construction of the Career Woman. In M. Barnard (Ed.), *Fashion Theory* (2nd ed., pp. 285–296). Routledge. https://doi.org/10.4324/9781315099620-31

Hackett, L. J. (2020). Diversity and democratization of Dior in Australia: Social factors in fashion modification in the 1940s–50s. *Journal of European Popular Culture*, *11*(1), 21–35. <https://doi.org/10.1386/jepc_00010_1>

O*pen, Axial, and Selective Coding in Qualitative Research: A Practical Guide*. (2024, October 29). Delve. [https://delvetool.com/blog/openaxialselective](#0)

*OpenStreetMap*. (n.d.). OpenStreetMap. Retrieved May 21, 2026, from <https://www.openstreetmap.org/>

Sikarskie, A. (2020). *Digital Research Methods in Fashion and Textile Studies* (1st ed.). Bloomsbury Publishing. <https://doi.org/10.5040/9781350042537>

Takahashi, S., Yamaguchi, K., & Watanabe, A. (2021). A Novel Approach to Analyze Fashion Digital Library from Humanities. Towards Open and Trustworthy Digital Societies: 23rd International Conference on Asia-Pacific Digital Libraries, ICADL 2021, Virtual Event, December 1–3, 2021, Proceedings, 179–194. <https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-030-91669-5_15>

The Geographies of Fashion. (n.d.). Retrieved March 11, 2026, from <https://www.bloomsburycollections.com/collections/monograph>

*Voyant Tools*. (n.d.). Retrieved May 19, 2026, from <https://voyant-tools.org/>

*Tutorial: About \| Voyant Tools Help*. (n.d.). Retrieved May 20, 2026, from <https://voyant-tools.org/docs/tutorial-about.html#>

*Welcome \| Taguette*. (n.d.). Retrieved May 19, 2026, from <https://app.taguette.org/>

*Zotero \| Your personal research assistant*. (n.d.). Retrieved May 20, 2026, from <https://www.zotero.org/>

![](bibliography/line_star.gif){fig-align="center" width="650" height="23"}

## Fashion movements and fabrics in Australia

Bellanta, M., & Cramer, L. (2023). “Well-Dressed” in Suits of Australian Wool: The Global Fiber Wars and Masculine Material Literacy, 1950–1965. Fashion Theory, 27(6), 861–888. <https://doi.org/10.1080/1362704X.2023.2228009>

Craik, J. (2009). Is Australian Fashion and Dress Distinctively Australian? Fashion Theory, 13(4), 409–441. <https://doi.org/10.2752/175174109X467468>

Cramer, L. (2021). Relaxed Bodies and Comfortable Clothes: Reframing Masculinity in Post-War Australia. *Gender & History*, 33(2), 390–407. <https://doi.org/10.1111/1468-0424.12515>

Crane, D. (2000). *Fashion and Its Social Agendas: Class, Gender, and Identity in Clothing*. University of Chicago Press.

Oldham, P. “Nazz.” (2025). “First Blood”: The 1960s Origins of the Australian Sharpie Youth Culture. *Journal of Australian Studies*, *49*(1), 76–90. <https://doi.org/10.1080/14443058.2025.2449710>

Hallett, C., & Johnston, A. (2022). *Fabric for fashion : the complete guide* (2nd ed.). Laurence King.

Hannon, A. (2016). “Hippie” is a Transnational Identity: Australian and American Countercultures and the London OZ. Australasian Journal of American Studies, 35(2), 39–59.

Karthik, T., Rathinamoorthy, R., & Ganesan, P. (2015). Sustainable Luxury Natural Fibers—Production, Properties, and Prospects. In S. S. Muthu & M. A. Gardetti (Eds.), *Handbook of Sustainable Luxury Textiles and Fashion* (pp. 59–98). Springer. <https://doi.org/10.1007/978-981-287-633-1_4>

Maynard, M. (1995). “The Wishful Feeling about Curves”: Fashion, Femininity, and the “New Look” in Australia. *Journal of Design History,* 8(1), 43–59.

Sabin, R. (1999). Punk Rock: So What?: The Cultural Legacy of Punk. Routledge. State Library Victoria *From flappers to fast fashion: 100 years of style in Melbourne*. (2022, June 4). <https://blogs.slv.vic.gov.au/our-stories/ask-a-librarian/100-years-of-winter-fashion/>

Pearson, J. (2020). Smart, Casual, Unisex: Can We Have Gender Equality in Twenty-First Century Fashion and Dress? In A. Smith (Ed.), *Gender Equality in Changing Times* (pp. 227–245). Springer International Publishing. <https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-030-26570-0_11>

Sneddon, J. N., Lee, J. A., & Soutar, G. N. (2012). Exploring consumer beliefs about wool apparel in the USA and Australia. *The Journal of The Textile Institute*, *103*(1), 40–47. <https://doi.org/10.1080/00405000.2010.542012>

Tapsuwan, S., MacMillan, C., Moglia, M., & Gordon, S. (2025). The price of sustainability: Consumer preferences for natural fibres over polyester. *Journal of Cleaner Production*, *533*, Article 146921. <https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jclepro.2025.146921>

Crewe, L. (2017). *The geographies of fashion : Consumption, space, and value*. Bloomsbury Publishing Plc.

## Other

Twain, M. (1905). The Czar’s Soliloquy. *The North American Review*, *180*(580), 321–326.

University of New England, (2026, May 20). *Research and Professional Practice Project*. University of New England. (Australia). University of New England. <https://www.une.edu.au/study/units/research-and-professional-practice-project-hass301>

England, U. of N. (2026, May 20). *Researching the Past in the Digital Age*. University of New England. (Australia). University of New England. <https://www.une.edu.au/study/units/researching-the-past-in-the-digital-age-hinq302>

## Appendix A - Figure 10

*Full coding data*

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
|-----------|-----------|-----------|-----------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
| tag | number of highlights | number of documents | type | Natural | Synthetic | Ambiguous |
| fabric - denim | 31 | 4 | n | 31 | 0 | 0 |
| fabric - wool | 27 | 5 | n | 27 | 0 | 0 |
| fabric - cotton | 25 | 5 | n | 25 | 0 | 0 |
| fabric - linen | 18 | 4 | n | 18 | 0 | 0 |
| fabric - silk | 14 | 6 | n | 14 | 0 | 0 |
| fabric - knit | 12 | 3 | a | 0 | 0 | 12 |
| embellishment - embroidery | 11 | 5 |  | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| fabric - leather | 9 | 4 | n | 9 | 0 | 0 |
| fabric - velvet | 8 | 4 | a | 0 | 0 | 8 |
| fabric - crepe | 8 | 2 | a | 0 | 0 | 8 |
| fabric - chiffon | 7 | 4 | a | 0 | 0 | 7 |
| embellishment - chains | 6 | 2 |  | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| embellishment - safety pin | 6 | 1 |  | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| fabric - jersey | 5 | 3 | a | 0 | 0 | 5 |
| fabric - suede | 5 | 3 | n | 5 | 0 | 0 |
| embellishment - jewels/minerals | 5 | 2 |  | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| fabric - fur | 5 | 1 | a | 0 | 0 | 5 |
| embellishment - sequins | 4 | 3 |  | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| fabric - flannel | 4 | 3 | a | 0 | 0 | 4 |
| embellishment - pearl | 4 | 2 |  | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| embellishment - flowers (not pattern) | 4 | 1 |  | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| fabric - crochet | 4 | 1 | a | 0 | 0 | 4 |
| fabric - taffetta | 4 | 1 | a | 0 | 0 | 4 |
| embellishment - beads | 3 | 3 |  | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| fabric - lycra | 3 | 1 | s | 0 | 3 | 0 |
| embellishment - tassel | 2 | 2 |  | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| fabric - brocade | 2 | 2 | a | 0 | 0 | 2 |
| fabric - cashmere | 2 | 2 | n | 2 | 0 | 0 |
| fabric - georgette | 2 | 2 | a | 0 | 0 | 2 |
| fabric - 'natural' | 2 | 2 | n | 2 | 0 | 0 |
| fabric - organdie | 2 | 2 | n | 2 | 0 | 0 |
| fabric - organza | 2 | 2 | a | 0 | 0 | 2 |
| fabric - plastic | 2 | 2 | s | 0 | 2 | 0 |
| fabric - polyester | 2 | 2 | s | 0 | 2 | 0 |
| fabric - rayon | 2 | 2 | s | 0 | 2 | 0 |
| fabric - velour | 2 | 2 | a | 0 | 0 | 2 |
| fabric - courduroy | 2 | 1 | n | 2 | 0 | 0 |
| fabric - nylon | 2 | 1 | s | 0 | 2 | 0 |
| fabric - shantung | 2 | 1 | a | 0 | 0 | 2 |
| fabric - smocked | 2 | 1 | a | 0 | 0 | 2 |
| fabric - tweed | 2 | 1 | n | 2 | 0 | 0 |
| embellishment - applique | 1 | 1 |  | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| embellishment - ruffle | 1 | 1 |  | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| fabric - angora | 1 | 1 | n | 1 | 0 | 0 |
| fabric - bosun | 1 | 1 | a | 0 | 0 | 1 |
| fabric - cellulose | 1 | 1 | n | 1 | 0 | 0 |
| fabric - chintz | 1 | 1 | n | 1 | 0 | 0 |
| fabric - gauze | 1 | 1 | a | 0 | 0 | 1 |
| fabric - lurex | 1 | 1 | s | 0 | 1 | 0 |
| fabric - mohair | 1 | 1 | n | 1 | 0 | 0 |
| fabric - mousseline | 1 | 1 | n | 1 | 0 | 0 |
| fabric - sailcloth | 1 | 1 | s | 0 | 1 | 0 |
| fabric - satin | 1 | 1 | s | 0 | 1 | 0 |
| fabric - serge | 1 | 1 | a | 0 | 0 | 1 |
| fabric - snakeskin | 1 | 1 | n | 1 | 0 | 0 |
| fabric - synthetic | 1 | 1 | s | 0 | 1 | 0 |
| fabric - tulle | 1 | 1 | s | 0 | 1 | 0 |
| fabric - twill | 1 | 1 | a | 0 | 0 | 1 |
| fabric - viscose | 1 | 1 | a | 0 | 0 | 1 |
| fabric - voile | 1 | 1 | a | 0 | 0 | 1 |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| total number of highlights | 283 |  |  | 145 | 16 | 75 |
| total amout of categories |  | 61 |  | 18 | 10 | 21 |
